goal: audit_vibe_coding: start from a software idea such as build a HASHI Watchtower, record and drive the work all the way to a finished and tested product, including end-to-end flow and internal loops such as testing -> bug -> debug -> fix -> testing, and exit only when the whole idea is built and tested to be fully functional.
intent_summary: audit_vibe_coding is a recording-first product delivery superloop. It begins with an idea, captures product boundaries and non-goals, turns the idea into an implementation plan, tracks nested build/test/debug/fix loops, requires end-to-end validation evidence, and exits only when the product is implemented, documented, and proven fully functional in real use. The loop now treats safe live testing as a mandatory acceptance gate: after smoke/debug loops, the orchestrator must prove the product works in the real deployed environment via controlled live actions, external/user-path invocation, visibility checks, and repeat fix loops until green.

criteria: idea captured with product boundaries and non-goals
architecture keeps core minimal and features modular
Watchtower/rescue sidecar API is narrow, explicit, and auditable
Windows and WSL target handling is explicit and tested
internal testing-bug-debug-fix-retesting loops are recorded in events/issues/tasks
real end-to-end smoke test proves the product works in the intended environment
operator documentation and acceptance evidence exist
no known blocker remains for normal operation
safe live-test preflight identifies protected systems, allowed failure targets, rollback/restore path, and required external view before any destructive action
real live testing exercises the deployed product through the intended external/user path, not a local bypass or mock-only path
live tests include off/on or failure/recovery behavior when the product is supposed to supervise, rescue, restart, or persist
if live testing fails, the loop must restore service, record the failure, fix the root cause, rerun automated tests, and repeat live testing until green
exit requires live evidence from all required directions/views, including status/listing visibility and post-action health checks for affected systems

candidate_steps.title: Capture original idea, intended users, product boundaries, and explicit non-goals
Extract requirements and architecture constraints from Watchtower discussion records
Design minimal core with modular sidecar boundaries, narrow rescue API, and structured audit logging
Model explicit Windows, WSL, and remote target handling without guessing paths or runtime context
Break implementation into product phases, taskboard items, waits, issues, artifacts, and evidence checkpoints
Implement the smallest vertical slice that proves the end-to-end Watchtower flow
Run internal test loop: test -> bug found -> debug -> fix -> retest until green
Run real end-to-end smoke test and prepare for safe live acceptance in the intended target environment
Run safe live-test preflight: identify protected systems, exact target components, independent control channel, auth, dynamic port/view expectations, and rollback plan
Run real live acceptance test through the intended external/user path, including controlled failure or off/on behavior when relevant
Verify live visibility from all required directions: status endpoints, list views, LAN/WSL/Windows perspectives, health checks, and audit logs
If live acceptance fails, restore service first, record failure evidence, debug root cause, patch, rerun automated tests, then repeat live acceptance until green
Collect acceptance evidence: logs, test results, operator docs, and known limitations
Exit only after the whole idea is built, documented, and proven functional by automated tests plus safe live acceptance evidence

notes: 2026-05-16: Added mandatory safe live acceptance gate after Watchtower HASHI2/HASHI9 rescue testing showed smoke/final review alone was insufficient.
